INDIAN COMMERCIAL DIRECTORY 73a. a prominent part in the Society were the late Mr. Kalanji Gopalji, who supervised and collected the rent of the cottages belonging to the Society until the time of his death, and also the late Mr. /A. L. Billomoria, who contributed very liberally to all the funds and held a responsible position in the Society. The aim of the Society is the upliftment of the meiibers in cultural and religious fields, to further the national cause of the Motherland and National Institutions... The Society was also represented on the Transvaal British Indian Association. The following are some of the many activities of the Society — The Society, represented by the late Mr. Parshottani Vallabh Bhagat (Caaivman) and Mr. Prag Prema {Seéretary), was a signatory to the illuminated Address presented to Mr, Polak by the Transvaal British Indian Association. The Society also assisted The British Indian Association financially and morally. ‘A substatitial dofation toe the Patidar Boarding House at Surat (India) on an uhdertaking being given by Mr, Vithalbhai Mehta, that the students of every community would be permitted to reside in the Boarding House. A donation of approximately £200 to assist the erection of a Dharmashala at Unai, a sacred place in India. . This provides accommodation for the pilgrims of the community. A present of £20 which was given to Mr. Narctidas Vithaldas Damania, in appreciation for the services he rendered to the Hindoo Community by erecting a Hindoo Crematorium in Johannesburg. The Hindoo Crematorium was equipped with the necessary furniture by the Society: A dénation of £37 10s. Od. to the Orrisa Famine Fund. -(India.) A donation of £37 10s. Od. for the rélief of the victims of thé Jaliariwala Bag (India) (through Mahatmaji) A donation of £135 to the Tilak Swaraj Fund {through Mahatmaji)- A dotation of a purse of £50 to Pandit Ishverdatta Vidyalankara, -(Gurukur Kangari.) The purchase Of a Stand for the sum of £250 in the 12th Street, Vrededorp, Johannesburg. it was mainly through the efforts of the Society that Mr Dalpatram Vanmalibhai Darji arrived from India as an educated immigrant. A donation of a purse of £26 5s. Od. to Mr. Shastriji for the Servant of India Society. Inthe year 1927, the Society decided to erect a Hall on thé Vrededorp Stand, and although the members contributed to this fund very liberally, it was later argued by some of the members that cottages should be erected on the Stand. Thus a dispute arose in the Community, and the Society ceased functioning until 1933, However, thanks to the efforts of Mr. Govindbhai Chbibabhai, the Society then again started to function, and since then its activities were as follows :-—~ A’Drait of £49 3s. 0d. was sent to Babu Rajendraprasad, Patna (India) for the relief of the victims — DEPARTMENTS — CYCLES, CYCLE ACCESSORIES RALEIGH CYCLES MOTOR “ACCESSORIES JOHN BULL -TYRES GRAMOPHONES AND SEWING Agencies — MACHINES .— PRAMS, BeS. AL CYCLES TOYS AND FOLDING .CARS HERCULES. CYCLES Sole Agents for— of the earthquake in Bibar. In the year 1936 the Society decided to erect cottages on the Stand, and members contributed approxi- mately £900 towards this fund. At present the Society owns four cottages, and has utilised one of the cottages for the purpose of a Hall. Numerous meetings and other functions have been held in this Hall, which has been a tremendous asset to the Community. The cottages are let and are thus a source of income to the Pand. ‘The Society last year founded a Ubrary known as the Transvaal Darjee Mandal Library, which is, and wa be of great educational value and interest to the whole community. . The library is housed in the all. The Society has granted a scholarship of three hundred and eighty-seven rupees to Mr’ D.M. Dalal, who-is doing his M.A. Class at St. Xavier’s College, Bombay. CYCLE HOUSE CORNER MARSHALL AND VON BRANDIS. STREETS JOHANNESBURG Please Address’ Correspondence to P.O. Box 5765 Phones 22-4971 (4 lines) Tel. & Cable Add. ‘‘ELKAH”’ A’sum of approximately £115 was collécted from the members and handed over to the Lourerico Marques Bharat Samaj. The Society is also’ trying to discourage those old traditions and empty shibboleths in social matters which retard the progress of the community. The Society has completely justified its existence, playing as it does an important and valuable part in the cultural life ofthe Community. ‘Through its charitable and honest works it enjoys the confidence and respect of those whom it serves.

BOX 10 LOURENCO MARQUES Fabrica Nacional. de Velas High Class Perfumery and Candle Manufacturers 88 Avenida Paiva Manso, LOURENCO MARQUES (Portuguese Bust Africa) THE PROPOSED SCHOOL BUILDING OF THE COMUNIDADE MAOMETANA INDIANA (THE INDIAN MOHAMEDAN COMMUNITY), AT LOURENCO MARQUES ‘The photo is of the Muslim School to he erected at a vost of £4,000, and another £8,000 will be spent towards maintenance of the school. by building shops for revenue purposes. Origivall¥ started in 1934, the new school awill be the fruit of many years of labour of the Muslim Community of Lourenco Marques, An income derived from investments of £150 per month will be spent towards education. The languages taught will be Arabic, Urdu, Portuguese, English and ti. Portuguese will be compulsory. ition, on the top of the shops, there will be a library hall and a particularly for the use of passengers from the Union and Mocambique Colony. Out of £12,000 nece: , £6,000 has been already donated by the people of Lourenco Marques, a and Inhambane district. To complete the amount a committee of the following is formed: Abdool Sacoor Abdul Latif & Co., Aboobakar Abdul Rehman & Co.. Hassam Adam & Co., Essa Moossa & Co., Adam Seedat & Co. and Kassam Adam.

11a, S.A. INDIAN COMMERCIAL DIRECTORY THE VED MANDIR: erected for educational and social purposes by the Hindus at Lourenco Marques. It is open to all Indian children irrespective of creed. The building cost £5,000. and is the first of its kind to be erected in Portuguese East Africa. It was ‘formally opened by His Excellency the Governor-General of P.E.A. in 1938. “A veritable mine of Information about the Indian Community of the Union, South and Northern Rhodesias, Nyasaland and P.E.A.” THAT IS THE S.A.
